purpose: The authoritative schema and worked examples for initialization.json. Mandatory pre-read before any write — the validate-initialization-schema.sh hook enforces every rule and blocks malformed writes. Provides minimum-viable, full-Waves, and full-Chains examples plus a 13-item pre-write checklist and failure-modes-to-symptoms mapping. Exists to eliminate the class of bugs where wave headers render with no task cards.
gotchas: plan.json MUST exist FIRST — validate-plan-required.sh hook blocks writes to initialization.json unless plan.json is present in the same dashboard directory with non-empty context.prompt and tasks[] where every task has id, title, description, approach, files.
agents.length MUST equal plan.tasks.length — schema hook blocks if not.
Never invent or scan for a dashboard ID — use only the assigned id from DASHBOARD ID directive or SYNAPSE_DASHBOARD_ID env var. If unassigned, stop and ask the user.
task.total_tasks MUST equal agents.length and task.total_waves MUST equal waves.length — mismatch blocks the write and produces wrong stat-bar counts.
sum(waves[].total) MUST equal agents.length — every agent must belong to a counted wave.
Every agents[i].id must match regex ^\d+\.\d+r?$ (e.g., '1.1', '2.3', '2.1r') AND be unique AND its wave must match a waves[j].id.
Every agents[i].depends_on[k] MUST reference an existing agents[].id — dangling references make dispatch readiness unstable.
In Chains mode: chains.length>=1, every agent appears in exactly one chain's tasks[], no duplicates across chains. Even Chains mode requires waves[] (at least 1).
Empty agents[] with populated waves[] -> dashboard renders wave headers with ZERO task cards (the canonical 'invisible swarm' bug).
Wrong dashboard directory write -> swarm runs but user's open dashboard panel stays empty — they see no progress despite workers executing.
SYNAPSE_SKIP_SCHEMA=1 escape hatch bypasses ALL schema validation — use sparingly, only for scratch/non-standard writes outside a real swarm.
patterns: 13-item pre-write checklist: kebab-case name -> 'Waves'|'Chains' type -> ISO 8601 created -> total_tasks==agents.length -> total_waves==waves.length -> agents.length>=1 AND waves.length>=1 -> id regex match -> id uniqueness -> agent.wave references waves[j].id -> depends_on references existing IDs -> sum(waves.total)==agents.length -> Chains rules -> correct dashboard path.
Atomic full-file write: read -> parse -> modify -> stringify (2-space indent) -> write. Partial writes silently freeze dashboard.
Repair task IDs use 'r' suffix and don't break the regex: '2.1r' is valid.
Dashboard merges initialization.json (static plan) with progress files (dynamic lifecycle) — malformed init means UI cannot render running workers.
conventions: task.name: kebab-case slug only (lowercase letters/digits/hyphens, starts alphanum).
agents[].title: short verb phrase, ~40 chars max.
waves[].name: descriptive (UI prepends 'Wave {id}: ' automatically) — not just 'Wave 1'.
chains[].tasks: ordered array of agent IDs left-to-right tracing the dependency path.
history[]: empty array at creation — populated by archive-move, not at write time.
